'''Bane's Heart''' was a unique carmine [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|lightsaber crystal]] given to [[Darth Zannah/Legends|Darth Zannah]] by [[Gender/Legends|her]] [[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Master]], [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]], following the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan]]. She used it in her [[Double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|double-bladed lightsaber]], which she named [[Bane's Heart (lightsaber)|after the crystal itself]].

The lightsaber and crystal later came into the possession of a [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] murdered by [[Grievous/Legends|General Grievous]], [[Supreme Commander of the Droid Armies/Legends|Supreme Commander of the Droid Armies]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], either on [[Dica]] or [[Necropolis (Core Worlds)|Necropolis]]. Grievous stored the weapon aboard his personal starfighter, which was later recovered by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces and used as part of the [[N-K Project]]. The Bane's Heart lightsaber was wielded by [[N-K Necrosis]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] until he was killed by a group of [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] who took the crystal from his [[Death/Legends|dead]] body.

When tuned to be used in a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], it caused that lightsaber to release an electrical discharge and made it difficult for anyone other than the rightful owner to use the lightsaber.

==Behind the scenes==
Bane's Heart appeared in the [[2003]] [[video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony|Sony Online Entertainment]] and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:RotW" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™|archivedate=20111124020123}}</ref> It was an unique lightsaber crystal that gave a purple-red hue to the player's lightsaber, contrasting its stated color of "carmine."<ref name="SWG:RotW" />

Bane's Heart was part of a series of 19 unique lightsaber color crystals created in the context of the [[W:c:swg:Publish 9 Notes for 29 June 04|Publish 9]]'s Jedi system revamp<ref name="SWG:js">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=other|jedi_spam.stf}}</ref> in [[June]] [[2004]].<ref name="P9">{{SonyCite|url=recent_updates.jsp?page=Recent%20Updates|text=Publish 9 Secrets of the Force}}</ref> However, only one of those crystals — [[Sunrider's Destiny]] — was made available in the game.<ref name="SWG:CEL">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=general|Capture Enemy Leadership|Dathomir}}</ref> Bane's Heart and the other crystals were not introduced in the game at that time.<ref name="soe.lithium">{{SonyForumsCite|full_url=http://soe.lithium.com/swg/board/message?board.id=jedi&message.id=1286265|text=A perfect way to (re)introduce all named color crystals and flawless pearls: Chu-Gon Dar Cube!|archiveurl=https://www.swgemu.com/archive/scrapbookv51/data/20070213155714/index.html|nolive=1}}</ref><ref name="thehonorempire">{{WebCite|url=https://www.thehonorempire.org/web/forums/topic/8045-rare-color-crystal-colors|text=Rare Color Crystal Colors|archivedate=20250801192732}}</ref> Bane's Heart was finally added to the game with the release of the expansion pack [[Star Wars Galaxies: Rage of the Wookiees|''Rage of the Wookiees'']], on [[May 5]], [[2005]], as a reward for defeating N-K Necrosis.<ref name="SWG:RotW" /> It was therefore the second unique lightsaber color crystal available to players.<ref name="soe.lithium" /><ref name="thehonorempire" /> In addition, Bane's Heart was also made more widely obtainable with the release of the [[W:c:swg:Hotfix 21.4|Hotfix 21.4]] on [[October 20]], [[2011]]. Players had a small chance to [[W:c:swg:Rare Loot System|loot]] the crystal from enemies defeated during [[W:c:swg:Hotfix 21.3|raids]].<ref name="21.4">{{SonyCite|url=players/news_archive.vm?id=69225&month=102011|text=Star Wars Galaxies Hotfix 21.4 -- Thursday, October 20, 2011}}</ref>
